title: tarenna graveolens subsp. arabica
intro: Tarenna graveolens subsp. arabica, also known as Arabica Tarenna, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of India. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Tarenna graveolens subsp. arabica include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Tarenna graveolens subsp. arabica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
